Proteomic Data Analysis

5.3 Differential Expression

We are going to look how proteins are differentially expressed between genotypes through three main techniques:

  1. Protein expression heatmap- A heatmap displaying how abundant different proteins are between the genotypes

  2. Volcano plot- a dot plot showing the degree at which proteins are differentially expressed between groups and the confidence in that measurement (the results from the moderated t-test, p-value or adjusted p-value).

3- Gene Ontology (GO)- A method to determine if certain “groups” of proteins (gene ontologies) are together more highly expressed in one genotype when compared to another.

5.3.1 Protein Expression Heatmap

For our protein expression heatmap, we will display only the top 100 most significant proteins, as determined by our moderated t-test (i.e., we will be selecting the proteins with the 100 lowest p-values). In reality, you can choose however many proteins you desire or use another criteria like log2 fold change. I personally like to use lowest p-values across the entire experiment.

5.3.1.1 Prepare The Data

From our replicate comparison data frame, we will take the following columns:

  • Protein group

  • Sample

  • Protein abundance (log2)

protein_abundance_wide <- sample_level %>% 
  distinct(protein_group, sample, log2_abundance) %>% 
  pivot_wider(names_from = sample, values_from = log2_abundance) %>% 
  drop_na() %>% 
  column_to_rownames(var = 'protein_group')

From our t-test data frame, we will sort the adjust p-value column (feel free to use something else if you desire) and sort it so the lowest p-values are first. Then we will select only the first 100 rows (slice_head(n=100)), and make a vector of the protein group IDs of those 100 proteins (pull(protein_group)).

significant_proteins <- diff_expression_level %>% 
  arrange(adj_pval) %>% 
  slice_head(n = 100) %>% 
  pull(protein_group)

Make the protein abundance data frame, keep only the rows that match the gene names selected above ([significant_proteins, ]) and convert it to a matrix.

heatmapData <- protein_abundance_wide[significant_proteins, ]

5.3.1.2 Clustering

We will then cluster both the protein expression profiles and samples by the abundance of those 100 proteins.

In our plot, we will only be using the sample clustering, but if we decide to, we can also add the protein clustering now.

hclustGene <- hclust(dist(heatmapData), #Clusters the proteins by expression profile
                     method = "complete")

hclustSample <- hclust(dist(t(heatmapData)), #Clusters the samples
                       method = "complete")

5.3.1.3 Prepare the Heatmap Annotation

Like we did for our sample-level heatmap, we will need to make a series of annotation items to help us label of heatmap.

Sample Colors

The first thing we’ll do is create a tibble (prot_htmp_sample_columns) containing all the sample information. Specifically, the names of the samples (sample) and the genotype of that sample (group). Note: the text in the sample column has to match EXACTLY the text of the sample names in the matrix heatmapData while the group names can be whatever you want.

prot_htmp_sample_columns <- tibble(
  sample = c('bisC1', 'bisC2', 'bisC3', 
             'msrA1', 'msrA2', 'msrA3',
             'msrB1', 'msrB2', 'msrB3',
             'msrC1', 'msrC3',
             'WT1', 'WT2', 'WT3'),
  group = c(rep('bisC', 3), rep('msrA', 3), rep('msrB', 3), rep('msrC', 2), rep('WT', 3)) #Can be whatever you want to set it as
)

prot_htmp_sample_columns
## # A tibble: 14 × 2
##    sample group
##    <chr>  <chr>
##  1 bisC1  bisC 
##  2 bisC2  bisC 
##  3 bisC3  bisC 
##  4 msrA1  msrA 
##  5 msrA2  msrA 
##  6 msrA3  msrA 
##  7 msrB1  msrB 
##  8 msrB2  msrB 
##  9 msrB3  msrB 
## 10 msrC1  msrC 
## 11 msrC3  msrC 
## 12 WT1    WT   
## 13 WT2    WT   
## 14 WT3    WT

We will then select a set of colors to label of samples, with the number of different colors equal to the number of groups (genotypes) we are analyzing.

  • In this scenario, we are going to create a color to label each sample, and there will be a total of 5 unique colors.
prot_htmp_sample_columns_colors <- hcl(
  h = seq(15, 375, 
          length = 5 + 1), #The number of total groups we have in our experiment
  l = 65,
  c = 100)[1:5] #Select the number of colors equal to the number of groups we have in our experiment

prot_htmp_sample_columns_colors
## [1] "#F8766D" "#A3A500" "#00BF7D" "#00B0F6" "#E76BF3"
Heatmap Colors

Next we need to define how we want the actual heatmap to be colored (i.e., the individual proteins).

prot_map_colors <- brewer.pal(n = 11, name = 'RdBu') #The heatmap will use 11 total colors from the "RdBu" color palette to span the abundance profile. Feel free to play around with this number to better fit your data
prot_map_colors <- colorRampPalette(prot_map_colors)(50) #We will span those 11 colors over a total of 50 ramps
prot_map_colors = rev(prot_map_colors)
Annotation Colors

Next we will define how each group (genotype) will be colored.

prot_htmp_groups_colors <- c(hcl(h = seq(15, 375, length = 5 + 1),
                                       l = 65,
                                       c = 100)[1:5]) #We take the same 5 colors used earlier for our group annotation and make them into a vector

#We will formally describe our group annotations here
prot_htmp_annotation_colors = list( 
  Group = c('bisC' = prot_htmp_groups_colors[[1]],
            'msrA' = prot_htmp_groups_colors[[2]],
            'msrB' = prot_htmp_groups_colors[[3]],
            'msrC' = prot_htmp_groups_colors[[4]],
            'WT' = prot_htmp_groups_colors[[5]])
)

5.3.1.4 Make the Heatmap

Now that we have both our heatmap data, clustering data, and heatmap annotations, we can combine everything together using pheatmap() from ComplexHeatmap.

Note: There are a ton of different possible settings. So feel free to mess around with the plot using the various arguments (hint, ?ComplexHeatmap::pheatmap())

pheatmap(
  as.matrix(heatmapData),
  color = prot_map_colors,
  fontsize_row = 5,
  main = 'Top 100 Differentially Expressed Protein (Lowest Adj. p-value)',
  annotation_col = prot_htmp_sample_columns,
  cutree_cols = 5,
  heatmap_legend_param = list(title = 'log2 Abundance', at = c(15, 20, 25, 30, 35)),
  annotation_colors = prot_htmp_annotation_colors
)

5.3.2 Volcano Plot

Next we will work on how to display the results of the moderated t-test with a volcano plot. We’ll go over how plotting using adjusted and un-adjusted p-values change the plots, along with various options to display your plots, including both static and dynamic plots.

5.3.2.1 Using Un-adjusted p-value

5.3.2.1.1 Static Volcano Plot
ggplot(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
         dplyr::filter(log2fc > -1 |
                         log2fc < 1 &
                         pval > 0.05)) +
  # Plot all genes
  geom_point(aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = '#9da7bf') +
  # Plot up-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc >= 1 &
                               pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = "#082544",
             size = 2) +
  # Plot down-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc <= -1 &
                               pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = "#DE3C37",
             size = 2) +
  # Add cut-off lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed') +
  # Draw each comparison separately
  facet_wrap(~comparison, scales = 'free') +
  # Add labels and themes
  labs(x = 'log2 Fold Change',
       y = 'p-value (-log10)') +
  plot_theme

ggplot(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
         dplyr::filter(log2fc > -1 |
                         log2fc < 1 &
                         pval > 0.05)) +
  # Plot all genes
  geom_point(aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = '#9da7bf') +
  # Plot up-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc >= 1 &
                               pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = "#082544",
             size = 2) +
  # Plot down-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc <= -1 &
                               pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = "#DE3C37",
             size = 2) +
  # Add cut-off lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed') +
  # Draw each comparison separately
  facet_wrap_paginate(~ comparison, 
                       ncol = 1, 
                       nrow = 4) +
  # Add labels and themes
  labs(x = 'log2 Fold Change',
       y = 'p-value (-log10)') +
  plot_theme

ggplot(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
         dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
         dplyr::filter(log2fc > -1 |
                         log2fc < 1 &
                         pval > 0.05)) +
  # Plot all genes
  geom_point(aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = '#9da7bf') +
  # Plot up-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc >= 1 &
                               pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = "#082544",
             size = 2) +
  # Plot down-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc <= -1 &
                               pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
             color = "#DE3C37",
             size = 2) +
  # Add cut-off lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed') +
  # Add labels and themes
  labs(x = 'log2 Fold Change',
       y = 'p-value (-log10)',
       title = 'WT/msrA') +
  plot_theme

5.3.2.1.2 Interactive Volcano Plot
ggplotly(
  ggplot(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
           dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
           dplyr::filter(log2fc > -1 |
                           log2fc < 1 &
                           pval > 0.05),
         aes(label1 = gene_name,
             label2 = protein_group)) +
    # Plot all genes
    geom_point(aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
               color = '#9da7bf') +
    # Plot up-regulated genes
    geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(log2fc >= 1 &
                                 pval <= 0.05),
               aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
               color = "#082544",
               size = 2) +
    # Plot down-regulated genes
    geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(log2fc <= -1 &
                                 pval <= 0.05),
               aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(pval)),
               color = "#DE3C37",
               size = 2) +
    # Add cut-off lines
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = 'dashed') +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed') +
    # Add labels and themes
    labs(x = 'log2 Fold Change',
         y = 'p-value (-log10)',
         title = 'WT/msrA') +
    plot_theme
  )

5.3.2.2 Using Adjusted p-value

5.3.2.2.1 Static Volcano Plot
ggplot(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
         dplyr::filter(log2fc > -1 |
                         log2fc < 1 &
                         adj_pval > 0.05)) +
  # Plot all genes
  geom_point(aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
             color = '#9da7bf') +
  # Plot up-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc >= 1 &
                               adj_pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
             color = "#082544",
             size = 2) +
  # Plot down-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc <= -1 &
                               adj_pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
             color = "#DE3C37",
             size = 2) +
  # Add cut-off lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed') +
  # Draw each comparison separately
  facet_wrap(~comparison, scales = 'free') +
  # Add labels and themes
  labs(x = 'log2 Fold Change',
       y = 'Adj. p-value (-log10)') +
  plot_theme

ggplot(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
         dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
         dplyr::filter(log2fc > -1 |
                         log2fc < 1 &
                         adj_pval > 0.05)) +
  # Plot all genes
  geom_point(aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
             color = '#9da7bf') +
  # Plot up-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc >= 1 &
                               adj_pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
             color = "#082544",
             size = 2) +
  # Plot down-regulated genes
  geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
               dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
               dplyr::filter(log2fc <= -1 &
                               adj_pval <= 0.05),
             aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
             color = "#DE3C37",
             size = 2) +
  # Add cut-off lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = 'dashed') +
  geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed') +
  # Add labels and themes
  labs(x = 'log2 Fold Change',
       y = 'Adj. p-value (-log10)',
       title = 'WT/msrA') +
  plot_theme

5.3.2.2.2 Interactive Volcano Plot
ggplotly(
  ggplot(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
           dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
           dplyr::filter(log2fc > -1 |
                           log2fc < 1 &
                           adj_pval > 0.05),
         aes(label1 = gene_name,
             label2 = protein_group)) +
    # Plot all genes
    geom_point(aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
               color = '#9da7bf') +
    # Plot up-regulated genes
    geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(log2fc >= 1 &
                                 adj_pval <= 0.05),
               aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
               color = "#082544",
               size = 2) +
    # Plot down-regulated genes
    geom_point(data = diff_expression_level %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
                 dplyr::filter(log2fc <= -1 &
                                 pval <= 0.05),
               aes(x = log2fc, y = -log10(adj_pval)),
               color = "#DE3C37",
               size = 2) +
    # Add cut-off lines
    geom_vline(xintercept = c(1, -1), linetype = 'dashed') +
    geom_hline(yintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = 'dashed') +
    # Add labels and themes
    labs(x = 'log2 Fold Change',
         y = 'Adj. p-value (-log10)',
         title = 'WT/msrA') +
    plot_theme
  )

5.3.3 Gene Ontology

The STRING database aims to collect, score, and integrate all publicly available sources of protein-protein interaction information, and to complement these with computational predictions. Its goal is to achieve a comprehensive and objective global network, including direct (physical) as well as indirect (functional) interactions.

Protein expression levels were compared between bulk samples and each purified cell type. This was done for both stained and unstained populations.

For all comparisons, proteins with a log2 fold change of greater than 0.5 in the purified population compared to the bulk sample were analyzed for enrichment analyses.

5.3.3.1 Map STRING IDs to enriched proteins

Proteins more abundant in WT than MsrA KO

E. coli taxon ID: 562

wt_v_msra_protein_list_up_wt <- diff_expression_level %>% 
  dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
  distinct(protein_group, gene_name, log2fc, adj_pval) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(log2fc > 0.5) %>% 
  arrange(desc(log2fc)) %>% 
  pull(gene_name)

wt_v_msra_protein_list_up_ko <- diff_expression_level %>% 
  dplyr::filter(comparison == 'WT/msrA') %>% 
  distinct(protein_group, gene_name, log2fc, adj_pval) %>% 
  dplyr::filter(log2fc < -0.5) %>% 
  arrange(desc(log2fc)) %>% 
  pull(gene_name)

head(wt_v_msra_protein_list_up_wt)
## [1] "yecF" "ygjK" "msrA" "cho"  "yieL" "mog"
wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt <- rba_string_map_ids(
  ids = wt_v_msra_protein_list_up_wt,
  species = 562
)
## Mapping 117 Input Identifiers to STRING Identifiers.
wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_ko <- rba_string_map_ids(
  ids = wt_v_msra_protein_list_up_ko,
  species = 562
)
## Mapping 59 Input Identifiers to STRING Identifiers.
wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt
##     queryIndex queryItem     stringId ncbiTaxonId            taxonName
## 1            0      yecF 511145.b1915      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 2            1      ygjK 511145.b3080      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 3            2      msrA 511145.b4219      511145 Escherichia coli K12

## 67          66      astB 511145.b1745      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 68          67      flgB 511145.b1073      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 69          68      motB 511145.b1889      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 70          69       acs 511145.b4069      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 71          70      tdcD 511145.b3115      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 72          71      puuC 511145.b1300      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 73          72      msrP 511145.b1971      511145 Escherichia coli K12

## 79          78      chiP 511145.b0681      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 80          79      glcG 511145.b2977      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 81          80      dppC 511145.b3542      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 82          81      yccT 511145.b0964      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 83          82      fimD 511145.b4317      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 84          83      thiP 511145.b0067      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 85          84      chbF 511145.b1734      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 86          85      putP 511145.b1015      511145 Escherichia coli K12

## 90          89      fabR 511145.b3963      511145 Escherichia coli K12

## 92          91      tdcC 511145.b3116      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 93          92      kefB 511145.b3350      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 94          93      chbC 511145.b1737      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 95          94      fimH 511145.b4320      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 96          95      cspG 511145.b0990      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 97          96      iraP 511145.b0382      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 98          97      puuA 511145.b1297      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 99          98      pdeH 511145.b3525      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 100         99      epmC 511145.b2326      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 101        100      fimA 511145.b4314      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 102        101      nanC 511145.b4311      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 103        102      ebgC 511145.b3077      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 104        103      dppB 511145.b3543      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 105        104      amtB 511145.b0451      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 106        105      sodA 511145.b3908      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 107        106      dppD 511145.b3541      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 108        107      dicC 511145.b1569      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 109        108      caiF 511145.b0034      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 110        109      ugpB 511145.b3453      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 111        110      allS 511145.b0504      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 112        111      dppF 511145.b3540      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 113        112      exbD 511145.b3005      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 114        113      panD 511145.b0131      511145 Escherichia coli K12
## 115        114      yegJ 511145.b2071      511145 Escherichia coli K12

##     preferredName
## 1            yecF
## 2            ygjK
## 3            msrA
## 4             cho
## 5            yieL
## 6             mog
## 7            mepM
## 8            frlB
## 9            dedA
## 10           flgG
## 11           cutA
## 12           hisM
## 13           prpB

## 24           ygjI

## 26           ycgJ

## 28           csgF

## 30           csgG

## 32           gfcE
## 33           yjcH
## 34           ibpB
## 35           fimB
## 36           uhpT
## 37           ygcB
## 38           yliE
## 39           actP
## 40           paaZ
## 41           paaF

## 43           ompG
## 44           astA
## 45           astE
## 46           yeeR
## 47           csgD
## 48           yhcN
## 49           paaH
## 50           astC
## 51           cadB
## 52           paaK
## 53           ddpD
## 54           adiY
## 55           uhpC
## 56           ydeP
## 57           aaeA
## 58           plaP
## 59           hipB
## 60           ydcU

## 63           paaJ
## 64           fadB

## 67           astB

## 69           motB
## 70            acs

## 99           yhjH

##                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  annotation
## 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   DUF2594 family protein.
## 2                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Alpha-glucosidase; Glucoside hydrolase that cleaves the alpha-1,3-glucosidic linkage in nigerose. Has very low activity towards maltooligosaccharides, soluble starch, nigerotriose, kojibiose and trehalose.
## 3                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Methionine sulfoxide reductase A; Could have an important function as a repair enzyme for proteins that have been inactivated by oxidation. Catalyzes the reversible oxidation-reduction of methionine sulfoxide in proteins to methionine.
## 4                                                                                          Endonuclease of nucleotide excision repair; Incises the DNA at the 3' side of a lesion during nucleotide excision repair. Incises the DNA farther away from the lesion than UvrC. Not able to incise the 5' site of a lesion. In vitro, the incision activity of Cho is UvrA and UvrB dependent. When a lesion remains because UvrC is not able to induce the 3' incision, Cho incises the DNA. Then UvrC makes the 5' incision. The combined action of Cho and UvrC broadens the substrate range of nucleotide excision repair.
## 5                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Putative xylanase; Protein involved in carbohydrate catabolic process.
## 6                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Molybdochelatase incorporating molybdenum into molybdopterin; Catalyzes the adenylation of molybdopterin as part of the biosynthesis of the molybdenum-cofactor; Belongs to the MoaB/Mog family.
## 7                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Murein DD-endopeptidase, space-maker hydrolase, septation protein; A murein DD-endopeptidase with specificity for D-Ala-meso- diaminopimelic acid (mDAP) cross-links. Its role is probably to cleave D-Ala-mDAP cross-links to allow insertion of new glycans and thus cell wall expansion. Functionally redundant with MepM and MepH. Partially suppresses an mepS disruption mutant.
## 8                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      fructoselysine-6-P-deglycase; Catalyzes the reversible conversion of fructoselysine 6- phosphate to glucose 6-phosphate and lysine. Functions in a fructoselysine degradation pathway that allows E.coli to grow on fructoselysine or psicoselysine.
## 9                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       DedA family inner membrane protein.
## 10                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Flagellar biosynthesis, cell-distal portion of basal-body rod; Protein involved in flagellum assembly and taxis.
## 11                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Divalent-cation tolerance protein, copper sensitivity; Involved in resistance toward heavy metals. Belongs to the CutA family.
## 12                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Histidine ABC transporter permease; Part of the histidine permease ABC transporter. Also part of a lysine/arginine/ornithine transporter. Probably responsible for the translocation of the substrate across the membrane. Required to relay the ATPase-inducing signal from the solute-binding protein to HisP (By similarity).
## 13                                                                                                                                                                                                     2-methylisocitrate lyase; Involved in the catabolism of short chain fatty acids (SCFA) via the 2-methylcitrate cycle I (propionate degradation route). Catalyzes the thermodynamically favored C-C bond cleavage of (2R,3S)-2- methylisocitrate to yield pyruvate and succinate via an alpha-carboxy- carbanion intermediate; Belongs to the isocitrate lyase/PEP mutase superfamily. Methylisocitrate lyase family.
## 14                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Putative uracil/xanthine transporter; Belongs to the xanthine/uracil permease family. Nucleobase:cation symporter-2 (NCS2) (TC 2.A.40) subfamily.
## 15                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Hydroxyisourate hydrolase; Catalyzes the hydrolysis of 5-hydroxyisourate (HIU) to 2-oxo- 4-hydroxy-4-carboxy-5-ureidoimidazoline (OHCU).
## 16                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Hca operon transcriptional regulator; Transcriptional activator of the hca operon for 3- phenylpropionic acid catabolism.
## 17                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                GNAT family putative N-acetyltransferase.
## 18                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  DUF1877 family protein.
## 19  Antitoxin of the ChpA-ChpR toxin-antitoxin system; Antitoxin component of a type II toxin-antitoxin (TA) system. Labile antitoxin that binds to the MazF endoribonuclease toxin and neutralizes its endoribonuclease activity. Is considered to be an 'addiction' molecule as the cell dies in its absence. Toxicity results when the levels of MazE decrease in the cell, leading to mRNA degradation. This effect can be rescued by expression of MazE, but after 6 hours in rich medium the overexpression of MazF leads to programmed cell death. Cell growth and viability are not affected when MazF and M [...] 
## 20                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Ring 1,2-phenylacetyl-CoA epoxidase subunit; Component of 1,2-phenylacetyl-CoA epoxidase multicomponent enzyme system which catalyzes the reduction of phenylacetyl-CoA (PA- CoA) to form 1,2-epoxyphenylacetyl-CoA. The subunit A is the catalytic subunit involved in the incorporation of one atom of molecular oxygen into phenylacetyl-CoA.
## 21                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Putative transporter protein.
## 22                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Gamma-glutamyl-gamma-aminobutyrate hydrolase; Involved in the breakdown of putrescine via hydrolysis of the gamma-glutamyl linkage of gamma-glutamyl-gamma-aminobutyrate.
## 23                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       CP4-57 prophage; Antitoxin component of a type II toxin-antitoxin (TA) system. A labile antitoxin (half-life of 2.1 minutes) that inhibits the endonuclease activity of cognate toxin RnlA but not that of non-cognate toxin LsoA.
## 24                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Putative oxidoreductase.
## 25                                                                                       Trimethylamine N-oxide (TMAO) reductase I, cytochrome c-type subunit; Part of the anaerobic respiratory chain of trimethylamine-N- oxide reductase TorA. Acts by transferring electrons from the membranous menaquinones to TorA. This transfer probably involves an electron transfer pathway from menaquinones to the N-terminal domain of TorC, then from the N-terminus to the C-terminus, and finally to TorA. TorC apocytochrome negatively autoregulates the torCAD operon probably by inhibiting the TorS kinase activity.
## 26                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Uncharacterized protein.
## 27                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Hybrid-cluster [4Fe-2S-2O] subunit of anaerobic terminal reductases; Catalyzes the reduction of hydroxylamine to form NH(3) and H(2)O. Is also able to reduce hydroxylamine analogs such as methylhydroxylamine and hydroxyquinone. Might have a role as a scavenger of potentially toxic by-products of nitrate metabolism. Belongs to the HCP family.
## 28                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Curli nucleation outer membrane protein; May be involved in the biogenesis of curli organelles.
## 29                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           1,2-epoxyphenylacetyl-CoA isomerase, oxepin-CoA-forming; Catalyzes the reversible conversion of the epoxide to 2- oxepin-2(3H)-ylideneacetyl-CoA (oxepin-CoA).
## 30                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Curli production assembly/transport outer membrane lipoprotein; May be involved in the biogenesis of curli organelles.
## 31                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Outer membrane porin PhoE; Uptake of inorganic phosphate, phosphorylated compounds, and some other negatively charged solutes; Belongs to the Gram-negative porin family.
## 32                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Putative O-antigen capsule outer membrane auxillary protein export channel; May be involved in polysaccharide transport; Belongs to the BexD/CtrA/VexA family.
## 33                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    DUF485 family inner membrane protein.
## 34                                                                                                                                                                                                              Heat shock chaperone; Associates with aggregated proteins, together with IbpA, to stabilize and protect them from irreversible denaturation and extensive proteolysis during heat shock and oxidative stress. Aggregated proteins bound to the IbpAB complex are more efficiently refolded and reactivated by the ATP-dependent chaperone systems ClpB and DnaK/DnaJ/GrpE. Its activity is ATP-independent.
## 35                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Tyrosine recombinase/inversion of on/off regulator of fimA; FimB is one of the 2 regulatory proteins which control the phase variation of type 1 fimbriae in E.coli. These proteins mediate the periodic inversion of a 300bp DNA segment that harbors the promoter for the fimbrial structural gene, FimA. FimB switches FimA on.
## 36                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Hexose phosphate transporter; Mediates the exchange of external hexose 6-phosphate and internal inorganic phosphate. Can transport glucose-6-phosphate, fructose-6-phosphate and mannose-6-phosphate. Also catalyzes the neutral exchange of internal and external phosphate.
## 37  Cascade complex anti-viral R-loop helicase-annealase Cas3; CRISPR (clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeat), is an adaptive immune system that provides protection against mobile genetic elements (viruses, transposable elements and conjugative plasmids). CRISPR clusters contain sequences complementary to antecedent mobile elements and target invading nucleic acids. CRISPR clusters are transcribed and processed into CRISPR RNA (crRNA). Cas3 plus Cascade participate in CRISPR interference, the third stage of CRISPR immunity; In the N-terminal section; belongs to the CRISP [...] 
## 38                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Putative membrane-anchored cyclic-di-GMP phosphodiesterase; Phosphodiesterase (PDE) that catalyzes the hydrolysis of cyclic-di-GMP (c-di-GMP) to 5'-pGpG (By similarity). Overexpression reduces biofilm formation. Cyclic-di-GMP is a second messenger which controls cell surface-associated traits in bacteria.
## 39                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Acetate transporter; Transports acetate. Also able to transport glycolate.
## 40  oxepin-CoA hydrolase and 3-oxo-5,6-dehydrosuberyl-CoA semialdehyde dehydrogenase; Catalyzes the hydrolytic ring cleavage of 2-oxepin-2(3H)- ylideneacetyl-CoA (oxepin-CoA) via the open-chain aldehyde intermediate to yield 3-oxo-5,6-dehydrosuberyl-CoA. The enzyme consists of a C- terminal (R)-specific enoyl-CoA hydratase domain (formerly MaoC) that cleaves the ring and produces the highly reactive 3-oxo-5,6- dehydrosuberyl-CoA semialdehyde and an N-terminal NADP-dependent aldehyde dehydrogenase domain that oxidizes the aldehyde to 3-oxo-5,6- dehydrosuberyl-CoA. Can also use crotonyl-CoA  [...] 
## 41                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      2,3-dehydroadipyl-CoA hydratase; Catalyzes the reversible conversion of enzymatically produced 2,3-dehydroadipyl-CoA into 3-hydroxyadipyl-CoA. Belongs to the enoyl-CoA hydratase/isomerase family.
## 42                    Anaerobic nitric oxide reductase flavorubredoxin; Anaerobic nitric oxide reductase; uses NADH to detoxify nitric oxide (NO), protecting several 4Fe-4S NO-sensitive enzymes. Has at least 2 reductase partners, only one of which (NorW, flavorubredoxin reductase) has been identified. NO probably binds to the di-iron center; electrons enter from the reductase at rubredoxin and are transferred sequentially to the FMN center and the di-iron center. Also able to function as an aerobic oxygen reductase; In the N-terminal section; belongs to the zinc metallo- hydrolase group 3 family.
## 43                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Outer membrane porin G; Forms channels functionally larger than those of classical porins.
## 44                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Arginine succinyltransferase; Catalyzes the transfer of succinyl-CoA to arginine to produce N(2)-succinylarginine.
## 45                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Succinylglutamate desuccinylase; Transforms N(2)-succinylglutamate into succinate and glutamate; Belongs to the AspA/AstE family. Succinylglutamate desuccinylase subfamily.
## 46                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              CP4-44 prophage; putative membrane protein.
## 47                                                                                                                                                    csgBAC operon transcriptional regulator; The master regulator for adhesive curli fimbriae expression; necessary for transcription of the csgBAC/ymdA operon. Plays a positive role in biofilm formation. May have the capability to respond to starvation and/or high cell density by activating csgBA transcription. Low-level constitutive expression confers an adherent curli fimbriae- expressing phenotype, up-regulates 10 genes and down-regulates 14 others.
## 48                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 Cadmium and peroxide resistance protein, stress-induced.
## 49                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      3-hydroxyadipyl-CoA dehydrogenase, NAD+-dependent; Catalyzes the oxidation of 3-hydroxyadipyl-CoA to yield 3- oxoadipyl-CoA; Belongs to the 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase family.
## 50                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Succinylornithine transaminase, PLP-dependent; Catalyzes the transamination of N(2)-succinylornithine and alpha-ketoglutarate into N(2)-succinylglutamate semialdehyde and glutamate. Can also act as an acetylornithine aminotransferase. Belongs to the class-III pyridoxal-phosphate-dependent aminotransferase family. AstC subfamily.
## 51                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Putative lysine/cadaverine transporter; Probable cadaverine/lysine antiporter or part of it.
## 52                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                phenylacetyl-CoA ligase; Catalyzes the activation of phenylacetic acid (PA) to phenylacetyl-CoA (PA-CoA).
## 53                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             D,D-dipeptide ABC transporter ATPase; Part of the ABC transporter complex DdpABCDF, which is probably involved in D,D-dipeptide transport. Probably responsible for energy coupling to the transport system.
## 54                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     Adi system transcriptional activator; Putative ARAC-type regulatory protein; Protein involved in transcription activator activity and transcription.
## 55                                                                                                                                                                                                     Membrane protein regulates uhpT expression; Part of the UhpABC signaling cascade that controls the expression of the hexose phosphate transporter UhpT. UhpC senses external glucose-6-phosphate and interacts with the histidine kinase UhpB, leading to the stimulation of the autokinase activity of UhpB. Belongs to the major facilitator superfamily. Organophosphate:Pi antiporter (OPA) (TC 2.A.1.4) family.
## 56                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Putative oxidoreductase; Probably involved in acid resistance. Belongs to the prokaryotic molybdopterin-containing oxidoreductase family.
## 57                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           P-hydroxybenzoic acid efflux system component; Forms an efflux pump with AaeB.
## 58                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Putrescine importer, low affinity; Putrescine importer. Required for induction of type 1 pili- driven surface motility.
## 59                                                                                            Antitoxin of HipAB toxin-antitoxin system; Antitoxin component of a type II toxin-antitoxin (TA) system. Neutralizes the toxic effect of cognate toxin HipA. Also neutralizes the toxic effect of non-cognate toxin YjjJ. Binds to operator sites with the consensus sequence 5-'TATCCN(8)GGATA-3' to repress the hipBA operon promoter ; binding of HipB(2) to DNA induces a 70 degree bend. This forces HipA dimerization, which blocks HipA's active site and thus its toxic action. May play a role in biofilm formation.
## 60                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Putative ABC transporter permease; Probably part of the ABC transporter complex YdcSTUV. Probably responsible for the translocation of the substrate across the membrane.
## 61                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     3-ketoacyl-CoA thiolase (thiolase I); Catalyzes the final step of fatty acid oxidation in which acetyl-CoA is released and the CoA ester of a fatty acid two carbons shorter is formed. Involved in the aerobic and anaerobic degradation of long-chain fatty acids.
## 62                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Putative drug efflux system protein; Confers resistance to fosfomycin and deoxycholate. Belongs to the major facilitator superfamily. DHA1 family. MdtG (TC 2.A.1.2.20) subfamily.
## 63                                                                                                                                                                                       3-oxoadipyl-CoA/3-oxo-5,6-dehydrosuberyl-CoA thiolase; Catalyzes the thiolytic cleavage of the beta-keto C8 intermediate 3-oxo-5,6-dehydrosuberyl-CoA with CoA to yield the C6 intermediate 2,3-dehydroadipyl-CoA and acetyl-CoA. Besides it catalyzes also the last step of the pathway, in which 3-oxoadipyl-CoA similarly is cleaved to acetyl-CoA and succinyl-CoA. Belongs to the thiolase-like superfamily. Thiolase family.
## 64                                                                                                                                                                      Enoyl-CoA hydratase/Delta(3)-cis-Delta(2)-trans-enoyl-CoA isomerase/3-hydroxybutyryl-CoA epimerase; Involved in the aerobic and anaerobic degradation of long- chain fatty acids via beta-oxidation cycle. Catalyzes the formation of 3-oxoacyl-CoA from enoyl-CoA via L-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA. It can also use D-3-hydroxyacyl-CoA and cis-3-enoyl-CoA as substrate. In the C-terminal section; belongs to the 3-hydroxyacyl-CoA dehydrogenase family.
## 65                                                                                                                                                                                                                     Succinylglutamic semialdehyde dehydrogenase; Catalyzes the NAD-dependent reduction of succinylglutamate semialdehyde into succinylglutamate. Also shows activity with decanal or succinic semialdehyde as the electron donor and NAD as the electron acceptor. No activity is detected with NADP as the electron acceptor. Therefore, is an aldehyde dehydrogenase with broad substrate specificity.
## 66                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Flagellar hook-length control protein; Controls the length of the flagellar hook; Belongs to the FliK family.
## 67                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Succinylarginine dihydrolase; Catalyzes the hydrolysis of N(2)-succinylarginine into N(2)- succinylornithine, ammonia and CO(2).
## 68                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Flagellar component of cell-proximal portion of basal-body rod; Structural component of flagellum, the bacterial motility apparatus. Part of the rod structure of flagellar basal body (By similarity).
## 69                                                                                                  Protein that enables flagellar motor rotation; MotA and MotB comprise the stator element of the flagellar motor complex. Required for the rotation of the flagellar motor. Probably a linker that fastens the torque-generating machinery to the cell wall. Overexpression of this protein with MotA improves motility in a pdeH disruption, (a c-di-GMP phosphodiesterase) suggesting there is an interaction (direct or indirect) between the c-di-GMP-binding flagellar brake protein YcgR and the flagellar stator.
## 70                                                                                                                                                                   acetyl-CoA synthetase; Catalyzes the conversion of acetate into acetyl-CoA (AcCoA), an essential intermediate at the junction of anabolic and catabolic pathways. Acs undergoes a two-step reaction. In the first half reaction, Acs combines acetate with ATP to form acetyl-adenylate (AcAMP) intermediate. In the second half reaction, it can then transfer the acetyl group from AcAMP to the sulfhydryl group of CoA, forming the product AcCoA.
## 71                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Propionate kinase/acetate kinase C, anaerobic; Catalyzes the conversion of propionyl phosphate and ADP to propionate and ATP. It can also use acetyl phosphate as phosphate group acceptor; Belongs to the acetokinase family. TdcD subfamily.
## 72                                    Gamma-glutamyl-gamma-aminobutyraldehyde dehydrogenase; Catalyzes the oxidation of 3-hydroxypropionaldehyde (3-HPA) to 3-hydroxypropionic acid (3-HP). It acts preferentially with NAD but can also use NADP. 3-HPA appears to be the most suitable substrate for PuuC followed by isovaleraldehyde, propionaldehyde, butyraldehyde, and valeraldehyde. It might play a role in propionate and/or acetic acid metabolisms. Also involved in the breakdown of putrescine through the oxidation of gamma-Glu-gamma-aminobutyraldehyde to gamma-Glu-gamma-aminobutyrate (gamma-Glu-GABA).
## 73  Membrane-anchored, periplasmic TMAO, DMSO reductase; Part of the MsrPQ system that repairs oxidized periplasmic proteins containing methionine sulfoxide residues (Met-O), using respiratory chain electrons. Thus protects these proteins from oxidative-stress damage caused by reactive species of oxygen and chlorine. MsrPQ is essential for the maintenance of envelope integrity under bleach stress, rescuing a wide series of structurally unrelated periplasmic proteins from methionine oxidation, including the primary periplasmic chaperone SurA and the lipoprotein Pal. The catalytic subunit Ms [...] 
## 74                     2-methylcitrate dehydratase; Involved in the catabolism of short chain fatty acids (SCFA) via the tricarboxylic acid (TCA)(acetyl degradation route) and via the 2-methylcitrate cycle I (propionate degradation route). Catalyzes the dehydration of 2-methylcitrate (2-MC) to yield the cis isomer of 2- methyl-aconitate. It is also able to catalyze the dehydration of citrate and the hydration of cis-aconitate at a lower rate. Due to its broad substrate specificity, it seems to be responsible for the residual aconitase activity of the acnAB-null mutant. Belongs to the PrpD family.
## 75                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  DUF1496 family protein.
## 76                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Outer membrane porin 1a (Ia;b;F); Forms pores that allow passive diffusion of small molecules across the outer membrane. (Microbial infection) A mixed OmpC-OmpF heterotrimer is the outer membrane receptor for toxin CdiA-EC536; polymorphisms in extracellular loops 4 and 5 of OmpC confer susceptibility to CdiA- EC536-mediated toxicity; Belongs to the Gram-negative porin family.
## 77                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Qin prophage; Cold shock-like protein.
## 78                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Cytochrome o ubiquinol oxidase subunit IV; Cytochrome bo(3) ubiquinol terminal oxidase is the component of the aerobic respiratory chain of E.coli that predominates when cells are grown at high aeration. Has proton pump activity across the membrane in addition to electron transfer, pumping 2 protons/electron.
## 79                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Chitoporin, uptake of chitosugars; Involved in the uptake of chitosugars.
## 80                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       DUF336 family protein; Belongs to the GlcG family.
## 81                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Dipeptide/heme ABC transporter permease; Part of the binding-protein-dependent transport system for dipeptides; probably responsible for the translocation of the substrate across the membrane; Belongs to the binding-protein-dependent transport system permease family. OppBC subfamily.
## 82                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   UPF0319 family protein; Belongs to the UPF0319 family.
## 83                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Fimbrial usher outer membrane porin protein; Involved in the export and assembly of FimA fimbrial subunits across the outer membrane.
## 84                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Thiamine/thiamine pyrophosphate ABC transporter permease; Part of the ABC transporter complex ThiBPQ involved in thiamine import. Probably responsible for the translocation of the substrate across the membrane (Probable). Belongs to the binding-protein-dependent transport system permease family. CysTW subfamily.
## 85                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Phospho-chitobiase; Hydrolyzes a wide variety of P-beta-glucosides including cellobiose-6P, salicin-6P, arbutin-6P, gentiobiose-6P, methyl-beta- glucoside-6P and p-nitrophenyl-beta-D-glucopyranoside-6P. Is also able to hydrolyze phospho-N,N'-diacetylchitobiose.
## 86                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Proline:sodium symporter; Catalyzes the sodium-dependent uptake of extracellular L- proline. This protein is also capable of using lithium as the transport cation. Also catalyzes the uptake of propionate.
## 87                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  D-ala-D-ala dipeptidase, Zn-dependent; Catalyzes hydrolysis of the D-alanyl-D-alanine dipeptide. May have a role in cell-wall turnover.
## 88                                                                RpoS antagonist; During the post-exponential growth phase transiently interferes with RpoS (sigma S) activity without affecting expression of RpoS itself. It is probably not an anti-sigma factor as its overexpression is detrimental in rapidly growing cells where there is almost no sigma S factor. There is a strong overlap between Crl- activated genes and FliZ-down-regulated genes. FliZ acts as a timing device for expression of the genes for the adhesive curli fimbriae by indirectly decreasing expression of the curli regulator CsgD.
## 89                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Maltose outer membrane porin (maltoporin); Involved in the transport of maltose and maltodextrins, indispensable for translocation of dextrins containing more than three glucosyl moieties. A hydrophobic path ('greasy slide') of aromatic residues serves to guide and select the sugars for transport through the channel. Also acts as a receptor for several bacteriophages including lambda.
## 90                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Transcriptional repressor of fabA and fabB; Binds the promoter region of at least fabA and fabB, but probably not yqfA. Represses the transcription of fabA and fabB, involved in unsaturated fatty acid (UFA) biosynthesis. By controlling UFA production, FabR directly influences the physical properties of the membrane bilayer.
## 91                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             Tagatose 6-phosphate aldolase 1, kbaZ subunit; Component of the tagatose-1,6-bisphosphate aldolase KbaYZ that is required for full activity and stability of the Y subunit. Could have a chaperone-like function for the proper and stable folding of KbaY. When expressed alone, KbaZ does not show any aldolase activity; Belongs to the GatZ/KbaZ family. KbaZ subfamily.
## 92                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 L-threonine/L-serine transporter; Involved in the import of threonine and serine into the cell, with the concomitant import of a proton (symport system). Belongs to the amino acid/polyamine transporter 2 family. SdaC/TdcC subfamily.
## 93                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  Potassium:proton antiporter; Pore-forming subunit of a potassium efflux system that confers protection against electrophiles. Catalyzes K(+)/H(+) antiport. Belongs to the monovalent cation:proton antiporter 2 (CPA2) transporter (TC 2.A.37) family. KefB subfamily.
## 94                                                                                                             N,N'-diacetylchitobiose-specific enzyme IIC component of PTS; The phosphoenolpyruvate-dependent sugar phosphotransferase system (sugar PTS), a major carbohydrate active transport system, catalyzes the phosphorylation of incoming sugar substrates concomitantly with their translocation across the cell membrane. The enzyme II ChbABC PTS system is involved in the transport of the chitin disaccharide N,N'-diacetylchitobiose (GlcNAc2). Also able to use N,N',N''-triacetyl chitotriose (GlcNAc3).
## 95                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Minor component of type 1 fimbriae; Involved in regulation of length and mediation of adhesion of type 1 fimbriae (but not necessary for the production of fimbriae).
