Adhesin responsible for the binding to D-mannose. It is laterally positioned at intervals in the structure of the type 1 fimbriae. In order to integrate FimH in the fimbriae FimF and FimG are needed.
## 96                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Homolog of Salmonella cold shock protein; Protein involved in response to temperature stimulus.
## 97                                                                                anti-RssB factor, RpoS stabilzer during Pi starvation; Inhibits RpoS proteolysis by regulating RssB activity, thereby increasing the stability of the sigma stress factor RpoS especially during phosphate starvation, but also in stationary phase and during nitrogen starvation. Its effect on RpoS stability is due to its interaction with RssB, which probably blocks the interaction of RssB with RpoS, and the consequent delivery of the RssB-RpoS complex to the ClpXP protein degradation pathway; Belongs to the IraP family.
## 98                                                                                                                                                                                         Glutamate--putrescine ligase; Involved in the breakdown of putrescine via the biosynthesis of gamma-L-glutamylputrescine. It is able to use several diamines, spermidine and spermine. Absolutely essential to utilize putrescine as both nitrogen and carbon sources and to decrease the toxicity of putrescine, which can lead to inhibition of cell growth and protein synthesis; Belongs to the glutamine synthetase family.
## 99  cyclic-di-GMP phosphodiesterase, FlhDC-regulated; Involved in the control of the switch from cell motility to adhesion via regulation of cellular levels of cyclic-di-GMP (c-di-GMP). Part of a signaling cascade that regulates curli biosynthesis. The cascade is composed of two c-di-GMP control modules, in which c-di-GMP controlled by the DgcE/PdeH pair (module I) regulates the activity of the DgcM/PdeR pair (module II), which in turn regulates activity of the transcription factor MlrA and expression of the master biofilm regulator csgD. Effect on flagella is controlled via the c-di-GMP-b [...] 
## 100                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Elongation Factor P Lys34 hydroxylase; Is involved in the final hydroxylation step of the post- translational modification of translation elongation factor P (EF-P) on 'Lys-34'. Acts after beta-lysylation of 'Lys-34' by EpmA and EpmB. EpmC adds an oxygen atom to the C5 position of 'Lys-34' and does not modify the added beta-lysine.
## 101                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   Major type 1 subunit fimbrin (pilin); Fimbriae (also called pili), polar filaments radiating from the surface of the bacterium to a length of 0.5-1.5 micrometers and numbering 100-300 per cell, enable bacteria to colonize the epithelium of specific host organs.
## 102                                                                                                                                                             N-acetylnuraminic acid outer membrane channel protein; Outer membrane channel protein allowing the entry of N- acetylneuraminic acid (Neu5Ac, the most abundant sialic acid on host cell surfaces) into the bacteria (Probable). NanC proteins form high- conductance channels which are open at low membrane potentials and which have a weak anion selectivity; Belongs to the oligogalacturonate-specific porin KdgM (TC 1.B.35) family. NanC subfamily.
## 103                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Evolved beta-D-galactosidase, beta subunit; Required for full activity of the EbgA enzyme. Exact function not known.
## 104                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            Dipeptide/heme ABC transporter permease; Part of the binding-protein-dependent transport system for dipeptides; probably responsible for the translocation of the substrate across the membrane; Belongs to the binding-protein-dependent transport system permease family. OppBC subfamily.
## 105                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               Ammonium transporter; Involved in the uptake of ammonia; Belongs to the ammonia transporter channel (TC 1.A.11.2) family.
## 106                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Superoxide dismutase, Mn; Destroys superoxide anion radicals which are normally produced within the cells and which are toxic to biological systems; Belongs to the iron/manganese superoxide dismutase family.
## 107                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          Dipeptide/heme ABC transporter ATPas; Part of the binding-protein-dependent transport system for dipeptides. Probably responsible for energy coupling to the transport system.
## 108                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       Repressor protein of division inhibition gene dicB; This protein is a repressor of division inhibition gene dicB.
## 109                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      Cai operon transcriptional activator; Potential transcriptional activator of carnitine metabolism.
## 110                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        Sn-glycerol-3-phosphate ABC transporter periplasmic binding protein; sn-glycerol-3-phosphate and glycerophosphoryl diester-binding protein interacts with the binding protein-dependent transport system UgpACE.
## 111                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               allD operon transcriptional activator; Positive regulator essential for the expression of allD operon. Binds to the allD promoter; Belongs to the LysR transcriptional regulatory family.
## 112                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              Dipeptide/heme ABC transporter ATPas; Part of the binding-protein-dependent transport system for dipeptides. Probably responsible for energy coupling to the transport system; Belongs to the ABC transporter superfamily.
## 113                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    Membrane spanning protein in TonB-ExbB-ExbD complex; Involved in the TonB-dependent energy-dependent transport of various receptor-bound substrates.
## 114                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           Aspartate 1-decarboxylase; Catalyzes the pyruvoyl-dependent decarboxylation of aspartate to produce beta-alanine; Belongs to the PanD family.
## 115                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                Uncharacterized protein.
## 116                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 DUF1479 family protein.
## 117                                                                                                                                                                2-methylcitrate synthase; Involved in the catabolism of short chain fatty acids (SCFA) via the tricarboxylic acid (TCA)(acetyl degradation route) and via the 2-methylcitrate cycle I (propionate degradation route). Catalyzes the Claisen condensation of propionyl-CoA and oxaloacetate (OAA) to yield 2-methylcitrate (2-MC) and CoA. Also catalyzes the condensation of oxaloacetate with acetyl-CoA to yield citrate but with a lower specificity.

5.3.3.2 Interaction Networks

Below are a set of tables displaying interaction networks for each protein identified within a given comparison. Within each table, “Target Protein” is a protein from our comparison, and all the entries under “Interacting Protein” is each a protein from the dataset that is known to interact with the target protein.

The data has been filtered to have a Log2 Fold-change of greater than 0.5 in WT compared to MsrA KO (both for the target and interacting proteins) and to have the highest confidence for “interaction score”, meaning these two proteins are highly confident to be interacting partners.

reactable(
  rba_string_interactions_network(
    #ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt$stringId[1:100],
    ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt$preferredName[1:110],
    species = 528,
    required_score = 100
  ) %>% 
    dplyr::select(c('preferredName_A', 'preferredName_B',
                    'score', 'ascore')),
  groupBy = 'preferredName_A',
  columns = list(preferredName_A = colDef(name = 'Target'),
                 preferredName_B = colDef(name = 'Interacting'),
                 score = colDef(name = 'Combined'),
                 ascore = colDef(name = 'Co-Expression')),
  columnGroups = list(colGroup(name = 'Protein Names',
                               columns = c('preferredName_A',
                                           'preferredName_B')),
                      colGroup(name = 'STRING Interaction Scores',
                               columns = c('score', 'ascore'))),
  theme = reactableTheme(
    borderColor = "#dfe2e5",
    stripedColor = "#f6f8fa",
    highlightColor = "#f0f5f9",
    cellPadding = "8px 12px",
    style = list(
      fontFamily = "-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif"
      ),
    searchInputStyle = list(width = "100%")
  ),
  pageSizeOptions = c(10, 20, 30),
  defaultPageSize = 10,
  highlight = TRUE,
  resizable = TRUE,
  showSortable = TRUE
) %>% 
  add_title(title = 'WT vs MsrA KO: STRING Interacting Proteins',
            font_size = 23)
## Retrieving STRING Network interaction of 110 Input Identifiers.

WT vs MsrA KO: STRING Interacting Proteins

5.3.3.3 Functional Enrichment Plots

Below are plots showing the 10 most enriched GO terms when comparing WT to MsrA KO (i.e., the proteins belonging to that terms are more highly expressed in WT when compared MsrA KO). There are several different terms used including:

  • KEGG Pathways: Assigns functional meaning to genes at both the molecular and higher levels.

  • GO Cellular Component: The place in the cell the protein is active

  • GO Molecular Function: The biochemical activity of the protein

  • GO Biological Process: The biological objective the protein contributes towards. This process is accomplished through one or more ordered assemblies of molecular functions

  • UniProt Keyword: A controlled vocabulary used by UniProtKB that summarizes the content of a protein entry. The main difference between GO terms and UniProt keywords is UniProt keywords are developed specifically for proteins added to the UniProt database while GO terms describe gene and gene product attributes independently of any database.

KEGG Pathways
wt_v_msra_kegg_up_wt <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt$string[1:150],
  species = 562,
  category = "KEGG",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 150 input Identifiers.
wt_v_msra_kegg_up_ko <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_ko$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "KEGG",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
grid.arrange(textGrob('More Abundant in WT', 
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_kegg_up_wt),
             textGrob('More Abundant in MsrA KO',
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_kegg_up_ko),
             nrow = 4, ncol = 1, heights = c(1, 9, 1, 9))

GO Component
wt_v_msra_component_up_wt <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Component",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
wt_v_msra_component_up_ko <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_ko$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Component",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
grid.arrange(textGrob('More Abundant in WT', 
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_component_up_wt),
             textGrob('More Abundant in MsrA KO',
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_component_up_ko),
             nrow = 4, ncol = 1, heights = c(1, 9, 1, 9))

##### GO Molecular Function {-}

wt_v_msra_function_up_wt <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Function",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
wt_v_msra_function_up_ko <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_ko$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Function",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.

There were not enough genes that were up-regulated in MsrA KO to be able to enrich any members of this GO category. Therefore, we will not be able to plot it.

grid.arrange(textGrob('More Abundant in WT', 
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_function_up_wt),
             #textGrob('More Abundant in MsrA KO',
             #         gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
             #                   fontface = 'bold')),
             #rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_function_up_ko),
             nrow = 2, ncol = 1, heights = c(1, 9))

GO Biological Process
wt_v_msra_process_up_wt <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Process",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
wt_v_msra_process_up_ko <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_ko$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Process",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
grid.arrange(textGrob('More Abundant in WT', 
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_process_up_wt),
             textGrob('More Abundant in MsrA KO',
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_process_up_ko),
             nrow = 4, ncol = 1, heights = c(1, 9, 1, 9))

##### UniProt Keyword {-}

wt_v_msra_uniprot_up_wt <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_wt$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Keyword",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
wt_v_msra_uniprot_up_ko <- rba_string_enrichment_image(
  ids = wt_v_msra_mappedProteins_up_ko$string[1:200],
  species = 562,
  category = "Keyword",
  image_format = "highres_image",
  save_image = FALSE,
  group_by_similarity = 0.6
  )
## Retrieving STRING enrichment plot of 200 input Identifiers.
grid.arrange(textGrob('More Abundant in WT', 
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_uniprot_up_wt),
             textGrob('More Abundant in MsrA KO',
                      gp = gpar(fontsize = 8,
                                fontface = 'bold')),
             rasterGrob(wt_v_msra_uniprot_up_ko),
             nrow = 4, ncol = 1, heights = c(1, 9, 1, 9))
